questions.question: An attacker is trying to run a script to steal data, but it keeps failing. Find the error in their process.
questions.codeSnippet:
touch stealer.sh
echo '#! /bin/bash' > stealer.sh
echo 'cat /etc/shadow' >> stealer.sh
./stealer.sh
# Error: Permission denied

options.text: They forgot to add the shebang
They didn't give the file execute permissions with chmod +x

questions.explanation: Even with a shebang, Linux requires the 'execute' bit (+x) to be set before a script can be run with './'.

questions.question: A network scan shows a server is 'filtering' all traffic on Port 80. Which CIA pillar is the administrator trying to protect?
questions.codeSnippet:
# Iptables configuration
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 80 -j DROP
questions.explanation: By dropping all traffic to Port 80, the web service becomes unavailable to everyone. This is a measure to control Availability.

questions.question: Find the bug in this command that is supposed to search all log files for the word 'root'.
questions.codeSnippet:
ls /var/log | grep 'root'
# This only searches filenames, not file content!
# Intended: cat /var/log/* | grep 'root'

options.text: They are grepping filenames instead of file contents
They should have used sudo rm

questions.explanation: Piping 'ls' into 'grep' searches the list of filenames. To search inside the files, you need to 'cat' them first.

questions.question: Which OSI layer is misconfigured if you can ping an IP address but cannot load the website (Port 80)?
questions.explanation: If ping works, Layer 3 (IP) is fine. If Port 80 fails, the issue is likely at Layer 4 (Transport/Ports) or above.

questions.question: Identify the 'hat' of this hacker: They found a bug, didn't exploit it, but posted it publicly on Twitter without telling the company.
questions.codeSnippet:
# Twitter Post
Check out this Zero-Day in Microsoft Word! No one knows about it yet. #Hacking
questions.explanation: Exposing a bug without permission or following responsible disclosure is typical Gray Hat behavior (acting without malice but without permission).
